QueueAclsInfo is a Java class that encapsulates Queue ACLs for a particular user.

QueueAclsInfo has the following key functionality:
- It has a default constructor that initializes the queueName and operations to null.
- It has a constructor that takes a queueName and operations array as parameters and initializes the corresponding instance variables.
- It has getter and setter methods for queueName and operations.
- It implements the Writable interface, which allows it to be serialized and deserialized.

QueueAclsInfo has the following core logic:
- It uses the StringInterner class to intern strings, which can improve performance by reducing the number of objects created.
- It uses the Text class to read and write strings to a DataInput or DataOutput.
- It uses the WritableUtils class to read and write arrays of strings to a DataInput or DataOutput.

QueueAclsInfo has the following inputs/outputs:
- It has a queueName and operations array as instance variables, which can be set and read using the getter and setter methods.
- It implements the Writable interface, which allows it to be read from or written to a DataInput or DataOutput.

QueueAclsInfo has the following dependencies:
- It imports the StringInterner, Text, Writable, and WritableUtils classes from the org.apache.hadoop package.
- It imports the IOException class from the java.io package.
- It has no parent or collaborator classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The TaskTrackerInfo class contains information about a TaskTracker, including its name, blacklisting status, reason for blacklisting, and blacklist report.

CORE LOGIC:
- The class provides methods to get the tasktracker's name, check if it is blacklisted, get the reason for blacklisting, and get a descriptive report about why the tasktracker was blacklisted.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The class implements the Writable interface, allowing it to be serialized and deserialized.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The class does not depend on any other classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This code defines a class called DelegationTokenIdentifier that extends the AbstractDelegationTokenIdentifier class. It provides functionality for creating and managing delegation tokens specific to MapReduce.

CORE LOGIC:

The code implements the necessary methods to create and manage delegation tokens. It overrides the getKind() method to return the specific kind of delegation token, which in this case is MAPREDUCE_DELEGATION_KIND.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The code takes in the effective username of the token owner, the username of the renewer, and the real username of the token owner as inputs when creating a new delegation token identifier.

DEPENDENCIES:

The code depends on the AbstractDelegationTokenIdentifier class and the Text class from the Apache Hadoop library. It also imports the InterfaceAudience and InterfaceStability annotations from the same library.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The main purpose of this chunk of code is to create a MapReduce specific delegation token secret manager. The secret manager is responsible for generating and accepting the password for each token.

CORE LOGIC:

The code chunk does not contain any key algorithms or processing patterns visible in this chunk.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The code chunk does not contain any data flowing in or out.

DEPENDENCIES:

The code chunk depends on the following classes:

AbstractDelegationTokenSecretManager
DelegationTokenIdentifier

This class is used to store the parameters required for fetching logs.

The class has a constructor that takes in the required parameters and sets them as instance variables. It also has getter and setter methods for each parameter.
